$comment: The struct-body descriptor mechanism: <sys/time.h>'s `structs` surface declares `struct timeval` — since c83 (D-FFI-MACHO-TIMEVAL-TV-USEC-WIDTH) as per-format `variants`: elf {i64,i64}, macho {i64 tv_sec, i32 tv_usec + 4 trailing pad} (Darwin __darwin_suseconds_t = __int32_t; both sizeof 16). The semantic phase injects the selected variant (tag + field scope + compositeScopeByType) so by-name field access compiles and the layout engine derives offsets 0/8. exit 42 witnesses tag resolution + field access + correct offsets on the unix ABIs (elf x86-64 + arm64-qemu + macho — the small tv_usec=2 value round-trips through i64 and i32 alike; shipped_timeval_macho pins the macho WIDTH). NO windows-pe arm: <sys/time.h> is POSIX, declared `availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho]`, so a pe compile FAILS LOUD (c8 per-target availability). RED-on-disable: without the structs surface + injection (or with the macho variant deleted), `struct timeval` is unknown → compile fails. The release arm confirms the full shipped pipeline (the optimizer over the new struct GEP/Load shapes).
